title: Typed Error Taxonomy for Plugin Tool Error Output
consumer_purpose: Give plugin tool authors a canonical error factory (imported from @opencode-ai/plugin) and give the LLM a standardized vocabulary for error recovery. The plugin tool runtime and the Effect engine runtime are separate — this taxonomy lives in the plugin runtime.
claim_atoms: New errors.ts module in packages/plugin/src/errors.ts, exported from @opencode-ai/plugin
All agent-callable tools import makeError/ErrorCode from @opencode-ai/plugin (already their sole import)
Existing .error and .status fields are preserved exactly — zero breaking changes
Phase 1 ships with errors.ts + 3 proof-of-concept tools converted

content._synopsis: ORCHESTRATOR. Plan revision 2 synthesized from 5 parallel subagent reports (foundation, load-bearer, building-inspector, blueprint, zoning-board). Three major corrections applied: (1) file location moved from .opencode/tools/ to packages/plugin/src/ to avoid plugin discovery double-load, (2) explicit scoping of separate runtime domains, (3) convergence mapping to existing errorKind strings.

root_cause_analysis.primary_root_cause: Plugin SDK boundary: ToolResult = string | { output: string, ... } at packages/plugin/src/tool.ts:36 provides zero structural guarantee. No shared error module exists in @opencode-ai/plugin. Every tool author independently invents error shapes.
root_cause_analysis.causal_chain: SDK boundary constraint → no shared error module → 55+ ad-hoc patterns → fromPlugin() bridge passes opaque strings → wrapExecute() sees success, not failure → failure collectors see no errors → consumers rely on ad-hoc JSON.parse()
root_cause_analysis.design_decision: This plan addresses Hypothesis 2 (add shared error module) within existing SDK constraints. Fixing Hypothesis 1 (changing ToolResult type) would be a breaking SDK change and is out of scope.

load_bearer.key_finding: ZERO consumers parse the JSON structurally. All tool output goes directly to the LLM as opaque text. TUI reads engine Part.state (separate system). No tests assert on tool JSON shapes.
load_bearer.implication: Adding error_code is purely additive — no breaking changes possible. The LLM is the primary consumer.

building_inspector.critical_risk: File placement in .opencode/tools/ triggers discovery mechanism at registry.ts:395 — every .ts file is loaded as a tool. RESOLVED: moved to packages/plugin/src/errors.ts.
building_inspector.high_risks: Overlap with classifyError errorKind and normalizeErrorCode — RESOLVED: explicit mapping table in plan. Separate runtimes, no conflict.
building_inspector.medium_risks: toModelOutput visibility for LLM — RESOLVED: Phase 4 adds prompt awareness. Plugin SDK contract — RESOLVED: errors.ts is an SDK addition, not a type change.

zoning_board.convention_mismatches: No shared modules exist under .opencode/ — RESOLVED: moved to plugin SDK where shared modules already exist (tool.ts, index.ts). ErrorCode naming should match existing enums — CONFIRMED: PascalCase with string values matches ApplyPatchError enum pattern.
zoning_board.convention_matches: PascalCase enum naming, errors.ts file naming, error_code field in RuntimeEvent

file_location.reason: .opencode/tools/ is a plugin discovery directory — Glob scan loads every .ts/.js as a standalone tool. Adding errors.ts there would double-load it and attempt to register it as a bogus tool.
file_location.tool_import: import { tool, makeError, ErrorCode } from '@opencode-ai/plugin' — tools already import from this package, so zero new import paths needed

error_code_convergence.context: The plugin runtime (.opencode/tools/) and Effect engine (packages/opencode/src/) are separate runtimes. Their error taxonomies serve different purposes: plugin errors are LLM-facing strings; engine errors are Effect TaggedErrorClass instances.

mapping_table.description: ErrorCode values map to existing errorKind strings used by typed-result.ts classifyError(). This is for human understanding, not code bridging.

error_code_convergence.note: Taxonomies are intentionally separate. Mappings are informational for developers. The LLM sees both error_code (from plugin tools) and errorKind (from engine tools) and can reason across them.

error_module_api.deps: ZERO. Pure TypeScript. No Effect, no plugin SDK deps. Self-contained.

ErrorCode.type: export const ErrorCode = { ... } as const; export type ErrorCode = (typeof ErrorCode)[keyof typeof ErrorCode]
ErrorCode.reason_for_not_const_enum: Plugin tools are loaded at runtime via dynamic import. const enum inlines values at compile time, which would fail resolution in the plugin context. Object pattern with as const works correctly in all contexts.

values.name: NOT_FOUND
values.description: Resource does not exist (file, plan, symbol, item not found)

values.name: PERMISSION_DENIED
values.description: Action blocked by policy or forbidden operation

values.name: INVALID_ARGUMENTS
values.description: Input failed schema/validation (invalid JSON, missing fields, bad schema)

values.name: CONFLICT
values.description: Resource already exists or state conflict

values.name: UNKNOWN_ACTION
values.description: Action discriminator not recognized by the tool

values.name: INTERNAL_ERROR
values.description: Unexpected internal failure (I/O error, corrupted state)

values.name: CANCELLED
values.description: Operation was cancelled by the user or system

fields.status: string (optional) — preserved from existing output format
fields.recoverable: boolean (optional, default false) — true if retry may succeed

makeError.signature: makeError(code: ErrorCode, message: string, opts?: ErrorOptions): string
makeError.description: Returns JSON.stringify({ error: message, status: opts?.status ?? 'error', error_code: code, ...optionalFields }, null, 2)

parseError.signature: parseError(output: string): { error: string, status?: string, error_code?: string, hint?: string, recoverable?: boolean, details?: Record<string,unknown> } | null
parseError.description: Parses tool output. Returns structured object if JSON with .error field exists, null otherwise. Works on old-format (no error_code) and new-format output.

isError.description: True if output parses as JSON with truthy .error string

isSuccess.description: True if output parses as JSON without .error field (or empty .error)

phase_0_sdk.goal: Add errors.ts to plugin SDK and export it
phase_0_sdk.change_in_index: Add: export { makeError, parseError, isError, isSuccess, ErrorCode, type ErrorOptions } from './errors.js'

phase_1_poc.goal: Convert 3 representative tools to validate pattern
phase_1_poc.pattern: import { tool, makeError, ErrorCode } from '@opencode-ai/plugin' — add makeError, ErrorCode to existing import; replace JSON.stringify({error:...}) with makeError(...); replace JSON.stringify({error: `Unknown action...`}) with makeError(ErrorCode.UNKNOWN_ACTION, ...)

before_after.before: return JSON.stringify({ error: `Plan ${args.plan_id} not found` }, null, 2)

phase_2_pattern2.goal: Convert pattern-2 tools (already have status, just add error_code)

phase_4_prompt_awareness.goal: Add ErrorCode documentation to LLM system prompt so the model can use error_code for recovery decisions
phase_4_prompt_awareness.approach: Add a section to the tool result interpretation guide: 'When a tool returns error_code, use this taxonomy to decide your next action...'

resolved_risks.resolution: Moved file to packages/plugin/src/errors.ts — outside .opencode/tools/ discovery directory
Added mapping table between ErrorCode and errorKind. Taxonomies are intentionally separate (separate runtimes). No code bridging attempted.
errors.ts is additive to the SDK exports, not a type change. No contract break.

remaining_risks.description: 30 tool files + 2 SDK files = 32 files to touch across phases. Mechanical but wide.
remaining_risks.mitigation: Phased. Phase 0-1 validates pattern before mass conversion.

remaining_risks.description: The plugin SDK may need regeneration (npm build) for the new export to be available.
No existing tests assert on tool error JSON shapes. Tests must be created from scratch.
remaining_risks.mitigation: Phase 0 includes test file creation. Zero regression risk.

blocks: ErrorCode enum — 10 values, distinct, string values match names
makeError — valid JSON, all ErrorCode values, opts handling, undefined omission
parseError — round-trip, old format compat, null cases, whitespace
isError/isSuccess — mutual exclusivity, old format, edge cases
backward compat — JSON.parse retains .error; consumers unchanged
tool integration — plan.ts, roadmap.ts, fragment.ts error paths
edge cases — special chars, empty message, deeply nested JSON in output

test_strategy.bisect_script: scripts/bisect-error-taxonomy.sh — 4 checkpoints: (1) unit tests, (2) backward compat, (3) tool integration, (4) full suite
